---
title: "ME/CFS — A Comprehensive Medical Documentation"
listing:
  id: recent-posts
  contents: blog/posts
  sort: "date desc"
  type: grid
  grid-columns: 3
  max-items: 6
  fields: [title, date, categories, description]
  date-format: iso
page-layout: full
---

::: {.lead}
A comprehensive, open-access medical documentation of Myalgic Encephalomyelitis / Chronic Fatigue Syndrome — a severe, chronic, multi-system neuroimmune disease affecting an estimated 0.89--2.5% of the global population.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## Medical Disclaimer
This documentation represents literature synthesis, **not clinical advice**. All treatment decisions must be made in consultation with qualified healthcare providers.
:::

---

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-7}

## The Paper

A structured reference work spanning five parts, with an epistemic classification system (achievements, hypotheses, speculations, predictions, warnings, recommendations). Available as a navigable website and [downloadable PDF](loth2026-mecfs.pdf).

- **[Part I — Clinical Presentation:](part1-clinical/)** Core and additional symptoms, diagnostic criteria, disease course
- **[Part II — Pathophysiology:](part2-pathophysiology/)** Energy metabolism, immune dysfunction, neurological, endocrine, cardiovascular, gut-microbiome, integrative models
- **[Part III — Treatment:](part3-treatment/)** Symptom management, medications, supplements, lifestyle interventions, emerging therapies
- **[Part IV — Research Evidence:](part4-research/)** Biomarker research, clinical trials, mechanistic studies, epidemiology, controversies
- **[Part V — Quantitative Modeling:](part5-modeling/)** Mathematical models of disease dynamics using ordinary differential equations

Use the **sidebar** to browse chapters and sections. Each page has its own table of contents.

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-5}

## About the Author

**Ing. Yannick Loth, M.Sc. (Management)**

Patient-researcher living with ME/CFS. This work synthesises the scientific literature with structured epistemic rigour — separating what is established from what is hypothesised, speculated, or unknown.

[Read more &rarr;](about.qmd)

**License:** [CC BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/)
**DOI:** [10.5281/zenodo.19627450](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19627450)

:::

:::

---

## Recent Blog Articles {.unlisted}

Short, accessible articles exploring specific ME/CFS topics — mechanisms, treatment reasoning, and patient-facing explanations.

::: {#recent-posts}
:::

[See all blog posts &rarr;](blog/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
